steps.title: Find the median position for data set A
steps.content: Data set A contains 41 values. The median is at position (41 + 1) ÷ 2 = 21. Count the cumulative frequencies: value 0 takes position 1; value 1 takes positions 2–8 (cumulative: 8); value 2 takes positions 9–16 (cumulative: 16); value 3 takes positions 17–25 (cumulative: 25). Position 21 falls in the range 17–25, so median A = 3.

steps.title: Compare with the median of data set B
steps.content: Data set B removes the single 13, leaving 40 values. The median is the average of positions 20 and 21. The cumulative count for values 0–5 is: 0 (position 1), 1 (positions 2–8), 2 (positions 9–16), 3 (positions 17–25). Both positions 20 and 21 fall within range 17–25, so both correspond to value 3, giving median B = (3 + 3) ÷ 2 = 3. Since Median A = 3 and Median B = 3, they are equal. That matches choice C — C is correct.
